applyTaxAfterDiscount: If True, discount is subtracted first and then tax is calculated on the discounted amount. If False or absent, tax is calculated first and then discount is applied.
currency: The primary currency of the invoice as ISO 4217 text code (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'INR'), not currency symbols.
invoiceInfo.category: Category that best describes the nature of this invoice according to line items.
invoiceInfo.customerMemo: User-entered message to the customer; this message is visible to the customer on their invoice or transaction. Typically used to provide additional context, notes, or remarks related to the transaction.
invoiceInfo.customerNumber: The customer account number assigned by the seller (e.g., 'Customer #: 1485223').
invoiceInfo.documentNumber: The unique identifier or number of the invoice.
invoiceInfo.dueDate: The date by which payment is due, only if explicitly printed on the invoice. Extraction format should be exactly as printed on the document.
invoiceInfo.dueDateISO: The date by which payment is due in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
invoiceInfo.issueDate: The date the invoice was issued(only date). Extraction format should be exactly as printed on the document.
invoiceInfo.issueDateISO: The date the invoice was issued in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
invoiceStatus: The payment/fulfillment status of the invoice. Use 'paid' if fully settled, 'unpaid' if outstanding, 'partial' if partially paid, 'processing' if still being fulfilled, 'cancelled' if voided, or 'unknown' if not determinable.
isOverflowPage: True if this page is a continuation of a table from the previous page (no new document header, line items continue mid-stream). False if this is a fresh page.
lineItems[*].description: The name or short description of the item or service exactly as printed on the line. Other data like serial numbers, lot/batch numbers, model numbers, quantities, prices, discounts, tax amounts belongs in their respective dedicated fields.
lineItems[*].discountAmount.originalValu: Line item discount amount present in a dedicated 'Discount' column or as a subtraction from the line total. Extract only when discount is explicitly printed against this specific line, otherwise leave as null. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].discountPercent.originalVal: Line item discount percentage present in a dedicated 'Discount' column or as a percentage against this specific line. Extract only when discount is explicitly printed against this specific line, otherwise leave as null. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].itemCode: SKU, part number, or internal item code.
lineItems[*].lineTaxAmount.originalValue: Line item tax amount present in a dedicated 'Tax' column or as a tax value against this specific line. Extract only when tax is explicitly printed against this specific line, otherwise leave as null. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].lineTaxPercent.originalValu: Line item tax percentage present in a dedicated 'Tax' column or as a percentage against this specific line. Extract only when tax is explicitly printed against this specific line, otherwise leave as null. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].lineTotalExcludingTax.origi: The net amount for this line item excluding tax. May equal lineTotalIncludingTax if no tax is applied. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].lineTotalIncludingTax.origi: The net amount for this line item including tax. May equal lineTotalExcludingTax if no tax is applied. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].quantity.originalValue: The number of units. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
lineItems[*].serviceDate: Date the service or product was delivered for this line item. Extraction format should be exactly as printed on the document.
lineItems[*].serviceDateISO: Date the service or product was delivered for this line item in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
lineItems[*].unitOfMeasure: Unit of measure for the quantity (e.g., 'each', 'kg', 'box', 'hr').
lineItems[*].unitPrice.originalValue: The price per single unit; if both a unit price and a List Price(or List Selling Price (LSP)) are present, use the LSP. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
parties.customer.addressStructured.city: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. City or town name only, following standard U.S. addressing conventions where the city appears separately from the street address, state, and ZIP code. Extract only the locality value explicitly representing the city/town.
parties.customer.addressStructured.count: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Country name or ISO alpha-3 code (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR'), only if explicitly printed.
parties.customer.addressStructured.posta: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Postal or ZIP code.
parties.customer.addressStructured.state: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. State, province, or region code/name.
parties.customer.addressStructured.addre: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Complete street-level address as it appears in the document, combining all available street address components into a single field. This may include house or building number, street name, street type, apartment/suite/unit/floor, building or complex name, district, neighborhood, or sub-locality. Separate each address component using commas to preserve structure and enable downstream parsing. Preserve the original formatting and ordering from the source document whenever possible. **Do not** include city, state/province/region, postal/ZIP code, or country in this field.
parties.customer.email: The primary customer-related entity associated with the document, including the purchasing party, billed party, or end customer. Triggered by labels such as 'Customer', 'Buyer', 'Purchaser', 'Sold To', 'Ordered By', 'Bill To', 'Invoice To', or 'Remit To'. Use this field for the main external party receiving goods, services, or the invoice, regardless of whether the role is purchasing, billing, or consuming. The email address of the entity.
parties.customer.name: The primary customer-related entity associated with the document, including the purchasing party, billed party, or end customer. Triggered by labels such as 'Customer', 'Buyer', 'Purchaser', 'Sold To', 'Ordered By', 'Bill To', 'Invoice To', or 'Remit To'. Use this field for the main external party receiving goods, services, or the invoice, regardless of whether the role is purchasing, billing, or consuming. The name of the entity (person or company) as explicitely mentioned.
parties.customer.phone: The primary customer-related entity associated with the document, including the purchasing party, billed party, or end customer. Triggered by labels such as 'Customer', 'Buyer', 'Purchaser', 'Sold To', 'Ordered By', 'Bill To', 'Invoice To', or 'Remit To'. Use this field for the main external party receiving goods, services, or the invoice, regardless of whether the role is purchasing, billing, or consuming. The contact phone number of the entity.
parties.seller.addressStructured.city: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. City or town name only, following standard U.S. addressing conventions where the city appears separately from the street address, state, and ZIP code. Extract only the locality value explicitly representing the city/town.
parties.seller.addressStructured.country: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Country name or ISO alpha-3 code (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR'), only if explicitly printed.
parties.seller.addressStructured.postal_: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Postal or ZIP code.
parties.seller.addressStructured.state: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. State, province, or region code/name.
parties.seller.addressStructured.address: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Complete street-level address as it appears in the document, combining all available street address components into a single field. This may include house or building number, street name, street type, apartment/suite/unit/floor, building or complex name, district, neighborhood, or sub-locality. Separate each address component using commas to preserve structure and enable downstream parsing. Preserve the original formatting and ordering from the source document whenever possible. **Do not** include city, state/province/region, postal/ZIP code, or country in this field.
parties.seller.email: The entity selling the goods or services, including the seller's business details and any associated shipping, dispatch, warehouse, or origin address information from which the goods are shipped. This may include the seller's operational or fulfillment location, but must not capture carrier, courier, freight, logistics, or transportation provider details responsible only for delivering the shipment. The email address of the entity.
parties.seller.name: The entity selling the goods or services, including the seller's business details and any associated shipping, dispatch, warehouse, or origin address information from which the goods are shipped. This may include the seller's operational or fulfillment location, but must not capture carrier, courier, freight, logistics, or transportation provider details responsible only for delivering the shipment. The name of the entity (person or company) as explicitely mentioned.
parties.seller.phone: The entity selling the goods or services, including the seller's business details and any associated shipping, dispatch, warehouse, or origin address information from which the goods are shipped. This may include the seller's operational or fulfillment location, but must not capture carrier, courier, freight, logistics, or transportation provider details responsible only for delivering the shipment. The contact phone number of the entity.
parties.shipTo.addressStructured.city: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. City or town name only, following standard U.S. addressing conventions where the city appears separately from the street address, state, and ZIP code. Extract only the locality value explicitly representing the city/town.
parties.shipTo.addressStructured.country: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Country name or ISO alpha-3 code (e.g., 'USA', 'GBR'), only if explicitly printed.
parties.shipTo.addressStructured.postal_: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Postal or ZIP code.
parties.shipTo.addressStructured.state: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. State, province, or region code/name.
parties.shipTo.addressStructured.address: Address broken into semantically meaningful components. Complete street-level address as it appears in the document, combining all available street address components into a single field. This may include house or building number, street name, street type, apartment/suite/unit/floor, building or complex name, district, neighborhood, or sub-locality. Separate each address component using commas to preserve structure and enable downstream parsing. Preserve the original formatting and ordering from the source document whenever possible. **Do not** include city, state/province/region, postal/ZIP code, or country in this field.
parties.shipTo.email: Extract the shipment recipient party only when a 'Ship To' or semantically equivalent identifier is explicitly present in the document, such as 'Ship To', 'Deliver To', 'Consignee', 'Delivery Address', 'Recipient', or 'Destination'. Do not infer this field from the customer, billing, purchasing, sold-to, or invoice-related parties unless the document explicitly indicates they are also the shipment recipient. This field represents the physical delivery destination or receiving party for the goods. The email address of the entity.
parties.shipTo.name: Extract the shipment recipient party only when a 'Ship To' or semantically equivalent identifier is explicitly present in the document, such as 'Ship To', 'Deliver To', 'Consignee', 'Delivery Address', 'Recipient', or 'Destination'. Do not infer this field from the customer, billing, purchasing, sold-to, or invoice-related parties unless the document explicitly indicates they are also the shipment recipient. This field represents the physical delivery destination or receiving party for the goods. The name of the entity (person or company) as explicitely mentioned.
parties.shipTo.phone: Extract the shipment recipient party only when a 'Ship To' or semantically equivalent identifier is explicitly present in the document, such as 'Ship To', 'Deliver To', 'Consignee', 'Delivery Address', 'Recipient', or 'Destination'. Do not infer this field from the customer, billing, purchasing, sold-to, or invoice-related parties unless the document explicitly indicates they are also the shipment recipient. This field represents the physical delivery destination or receiving party for the goods. The contact phone number of the entity.
shippingInfo.deliveryDate: The expected or actual delivery date or shipping date. Extraction format should be exactly as printed on the document if a valid delivery date is fount, else null.
shippingInfo.deliveryDateISO: The expected or actual delivery date or shipping date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD).
shippingInfo.trackingNumber: Shipment tracking number (also labelled 'Waybill #', 'AWB', or 'Airway Bill' or 'Delivery No.' or 'Tracking No.' on some documents).
totals.balanceDue.originalValue: The remaining amount owed as explicitly printed on the document after all payments, credits, and deductions. Also labelled 'Amount Due', 'Balance Due', 'Please Pay', 'Total Due', or 'Net Due'. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.deposit.originalValue: Deposit or advance payment already paid and explicitly deducted on this invoice (e.g., 'Deposit Paid', 'Advance', 'Down Payment'). The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.discountPercentage.originalValue: Final Discount Percentage: Extract only when a total discount percentage is explicitly printed in the totals section (e.g., '5% discount', 'Less 10%'). The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.discountTotal.originalValue: Final Discount Amount: Extract only when a total discount amount is explicitly printed in the totals section as a monetary value, applied against the final subtotal (e.g., 'Discount: -$50', 'Less discount', 'Trade Discount', 'Promotional Discount'). only extract if an amount is printed. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.exchangeRate.originalValue: The exchange rate as printed on the document: number of home/reporting currency units per one unit of the invoice currency (e.g., if invoice is in USD and home currency is INR, this would be ~83.5). Extract only if explicitly stated. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.otherCharges[*].key: Dictionary of any additional non-line-item charges explicitly printed on the document that are applied outside the base merchandise or service amounts. Use the charge label/name as the dictionary key and the corresponding charge amount as the value. This field is intended to flexibly capture any extra charges that may appear across different invoice formats. Only extract charges that are explicitly mentioned or printed in the document and do not infer or calculate missing charges. Preserve the original charge label as closely as possible for the dictionary key. The name or label of the charge as printed on the document.
totals.otherCharges[*].value.originalVal: Dictionary of any additional non-line-item charges explicitly printed on the document that are applied outside the base merchandise or service amounts. Use the charge label/name as the dictionary key and the corresponding charge amount as the value. This field is intended to flexibly capture any extra charges that may appear across different invoice formats. Only extract charges that are explicitly mentioned or printed in the document and do not infer or calculate missing charges. Preserve the original charge label as closely as possible for the dictionary key. The amount of the charge as printed on the document. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.subtotal.originalValue: Extract the subtotal amount only when it is explicitly printed or clearly identified in the document using labels such as 'Subtotal', 'Sub Total', 'Net Amount', 'Net Total', 'Merchandise Total', 'Items Total', or other semantically similar identifiers. This value represents the total of all line item amounts before applying taxes, shipping, handling, surcharges, discounts, or other additional charges. Do not infer or calculate the subtotal if it is not explicitly present. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.taxAmount.originalValue: Extract the total tax amount only when it is explicitly printed or clearly identified in the document using labels such as 'Tax', 'Total Tax', 'Tax Amount', 'GST', 'VAT', 'Sales Tax', or other semantically similar identifiers. This field represents the overall tax amount charged on the document. Do not calculate, infer, or sum individual tax lines if a total tax amount is not explicitly present in the document or image. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.taxName: The explicit name or label of the tax associated with the extracted taxAmount, exactly as shown in the document. Examples may include 'Sales Tax', 'State Tax', 'County Tax', 'City Tax', 'Use Tax', 'VAT', 'GST', or other printed tax identifiers. Extract only when explicitly present and do not infer, standardize, or normalize the value.
totals.taxPercentage.originalValue: The explicit tax rate or percentage associated with the extracted taxAmount, as printed in the document (e.g., '10%', '18', 'VAT Rate'). Extract only when explicitly present alongside or clearly associated with the taxAmount. Do not calculate or infer the percentage from the tax amount or subtotal. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.totalExcludingTax.originalValue: The total amount after applying discounts and adding other charges (like freight, handling, insurance, etc.) but BEFORE any tax is applied. This is the taxable base. Typically labelled 'Total Before Tax', 'Net Total', or 'Taxable Amount'. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
totals.totalIncludingTax.originalValue: The grand total amount including all taxes, all charges, and after all discounts. Typically labelled 'Total', 'Invoice Total', 'Grand Total', or 'Total Inc. Tax'. The raw value exactly as it appears printed on the document (e.g. '1,234.56', '10%', '2 pcs'). Preserve bracket notation as-is (e.g. '(102.68)').
